heading: Polygraph testing prohibition
label: 10451 Polygraph testing prohibition
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 3796gg–8 of Title 42
Effective DateSection not effective until the beginning of fiscal year 2007, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2006 Amendment note under .section 4 of Pub. L. 109–162section 10261 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection not effective until the beginning of fiscal year 2007, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2006 Amendment note under .section 4 of Pub. L. 109–162section 10261 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: In order to be eligible for grants under this subchapter, a State, Indian tribal government, territorial government, or unit of local government shall certify that, not later than 3 years after , their laws, policies, or practices will ensure that no law enforcement officer, prosecuting officer or other government official shall ask or require an adult, youth, or child victim of an alleged sex offense as defined under Federal, tribal, State, territorial, or local law to submit to a polygraph examination or other truth telling device as a condition for proceeding with the investigation of such an offense.January 5, 2006

children.heading: Prosecution
children.label: b Prosecution
children.text: The refusal of a victim to submit to an examination described in subsection (a) shall not prevent the investigation, charging, or prosecution of the offense.
